title: Clibadium badieri
intro: Clibadium badieri, also known as "chuchuhuasi," is a medicinal plant native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits. Bioactive constituents of Clibadium badieri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction to treat a range of ailments, as well as being used in herbal remedies for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
taxonomy: Clibadium badieri is classified within the plant family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the order Asterales. Clibadium badieri also belongs to the genus Clibadium. The family Asteraceae is commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. Within the plant kingdom, Clibadium badieri is a member of the kingdom Plantae.
